campaigns.name: Operation Aurora
campaigns.description: Operation Aurora was a series of cyberattacks originating from China targeting major technology and security companies including Google, Adobe, and Juniper Networks. The attack exploited a zero-day Internet Explorer vulnerability and aimed to steal source code and access Gmail accounts of Chinese human rights activists.
campaigns.impact_summary: Compromised 34+ major technology companies; Google partially withdrew from China market; led to significant changes in corporate security posture across Silicon Valley

campaigns.name: SolarWinds Supply Chain (SUNBURST)
campaigns.description: The SolarWinds supply chain attack inserted SUNBURST backdoor into SolarWinds Orion software updates, distributing malware to 18,000+ organizations. Active exploitation of approximately 100 organizations including 9 US federal agencies followed. Attribution to Russian SVR was confirmed by all Five Eyes intelligence agencies.
campaigns.impact_summary: 18,000 organizations received malicious updates; ~100 actively exploited; US federal agencies including Departments of Treasury, Commerce, State, Homeland Security breached; estimated economic impact $90+ billion

campaigns.name: MOVEit Mass Exploitation
campaigns.description: Cl0p exploited a zero-day SQL injection vulnerability (CVE-2023-34362) in Progress Software's MOVEit Transfer application, conducting mass exfiltration without ransomware deployment. The campaign targeted managed file transfer deployments globally, exfiltrating data for extortion.
campaigns.impact_summary: Over 2,700 organizations affected; 93+ million individuals had data exposed; notable victims included US DOE, Shell, BBC, British Airways, Aer Lingus, PwC, Ernst & Young

campaigns.name: 3CX Software Supply Chain Attack
campaigns.description: North Korea's Lazarus Group compromised 3CX's build pipeline, distributing a trojanized version of the 3CX softphone application to 600,000+ customers. The attack was the first documented software supply chain attack leveraging a prior supply chain compromise (Trading Technologies X_TRADER).
campaigns.impact_summary: 600,000+ 3CX customers potentially exposed; financial and cryptocurrency firms in targeted sectors identified for follow-on intrusions; North Korean cryptocurrency theft estimated $100M+

campaigns.name: Kaseya VSA Ransomware Attack
campaigns.description: REvil exploited zero-day vulnerabilities in Kaseya VSA (CVE-2021-30116) to push ransomware through managed service providers (MSPs) to up to 1,500 downstream businesses. The attack exploited the July 4th US holiday weekend to delay response. REvil demanded $70 million for a universal decryptor.
campaigns.impact_summary: Up to 1,500 businesses encrypted; Swedish Coop supermarket chain forced to close 800 stores; initial $70M ransom demand; FBI obtained decryptor in July 2021

campaigns.name: WannaCry Global Ransomware
campaigns.description: WannaCry ransomware used the NSA-developed EternalBlue exploit (leaked by Shadow Brokers) to spread laterally through networks via SMBv1. The attack caused catastrophic disruption to the UK NHS, Telefonica, FedEx, and 200,000+ systems in 150 countries within days.
campaigns.impact_summary: 200,000+ systems in 150 countries infected; NHS UK operations severely disrupted with estimated GBP 92M damage; total global economic damage estimated $4-8 billion; 80 NHS trusts affected

campaigns.name: NotPetya Destructive Wiper
campaigns.description: NotPetya was a destructive wiper masquerading as ransomware, distributed via the MeDoc Ukrainian accounting software update mechanism. Unlike ransomware, NotPetya was designed for maximum destruction with no functioning recovery mechanism. The attack spread globally through corporate networks causing unprecedented damage.
campaigns.impact_summary: Estimated $10 billion in global damages; Maersk shipping lost $300M; Merck $870M; FedEx/TNT $400M; Mondelez $188M; devastated Ukrainian financial system; most costly cyberattack in history

campaigns.name: Colonial Pipeline Ransomware
campaigns.description: DarkSide ransomware group breached Colonial Pipeline, the largest US fuel pipeline, via a legacy VPN account with no MFA. Colonial proactively shut down pipeline operations causing fuel shortages across the US East Coast. The US DOJ subsequently recovered $2.3 million of the $4.4 million ransom payment.
campaigns.impact_summary: Largest publicly disclosed cyberattack on US critical infrastructure; US East Coast fuel shortages; $4.4M ransom paid; US government declared state of emergency; 17 states affected

campaigns.name: JBS Foods Ransomware
campaigns.description: REvil ransomware compromised JBS Foods, the world's largest meat processing company, forcing temporary shutdown of all US beef plants and Australian and Canadian operations. The attack occurred just weeks after Colonial Pipeline demonstrating the vulnerability of critical food supply infrastructure.
campaigns.impact_summary: All 9 US beef plants temporarily shut; Australian operations disrupted; $11M ransom paid; potential food supply chain disruption averted by rapid response

campaigns.name: Change Healthcare Attack
campaigns.description: ALPHV/BlackCat compromised Change Healthcare (UnitedHealth Group) via stolen Citrix credentials lacking MFA, disrupting prescription processing for 70,000+ US pharmacies for weeks. The group allegedly received $22 million ransom then exit-scammed its own affiliates. A separate group RansomHub subsequently extorted the same data.
campaigns.impact_summary: 67 million Americans had health data exposed; US pharmacy system disrupted for 2+ weeks; $22M ransom paid; UnitedHealth Group $872M financial impact in Q1 2024; critical healthcare infrastructure most impacted attack in US history

campaigns.name: MGM Resorts Cyberattack
campaigns.description: Scattered Spider used vishing (voice phishing) to social engineer MGM's IT help desk into resetting an employee's credentials, gaining initial access that enabled ALPHV ransomware deployment. Casino floors, hotel systems, and digital key cards were disrupted for 10 days across multiple MGM Las Vegas properties.
campaigns.impact_summary: $100M+ estimated financial impact; casino operations and hotel systems disrupted for 10 days; slot machines offline; ATMs non-functional; 10.6 million customer records compromised

campaigns.name: Caesars Entertainment Breach
campaigns.description: Scattered Spider breached Caesars Entertainment through social engineering of an outsourced IT vendor, stealing the loyalty program database containing Social Security numbers and driver's license numbers of tens of millions of members. Caesars reportedly paid approximately $15 million ransom (half the original $30M demand).
campaigns.impact_summary: Tens of millions of loyalty program members' SSNs and DLs exposed; estimated $15M ransom paid; SEC 8-K disclosure filed; data reportedly deleted per ransom payment

campaigns.name: HAFNIUM Microsoft Exchange Exploitation (ProxyLogon)
campaigns.description: HAFNIUM exploited four zero-day vulnerabilities in Microsoft Exchange Server (CVE-2021-26855, 26857, 26858, 27065 - collectively ProxyLogon) beginning in January 2021. Mass exploitation followed public disclosure in March 2021, with competing APT groups rushing to install web shells on vulnerable Exchange servers globally.
campaigns.impact_summary: 250,000+ Exchange servers compromised globally; 30,000+ US organizations affected; 9 nations attributed involvement; critical US government and defense contractor email systems breached

campaigns.name: Log4Shell Mass Exploitation
campaigns.actor_display_name: Multiple Threat Actors (APT41, Lazarus, Iranian APTs, Criminal Groups)
campaigns.description: CVE-2021-44228 in Apache Log4j (CVSS 10.0) enabled remote code execution via JNDI injection in log messages. Immediate mass exploitation by nation-state actors, criminal groups, and opportunistic attackers affected hundreds of millions of Java applications globally. CISA designated it one of the most serious vulnerabilities ever discovered.
campaigns.impact_summary: Hundreds of millions of applications affected; exploitation by APT41, Lazarus, multiple Iranian APTs within 24 hours of disclosure; Belgian Defense Ministry compromised; widespread cryptocurrency mining and botnet enrollment

campaigns.name: ProxyLogon Exchange Exploitation
campaigns.description: ProxyLogon refers to the chain of four Microsoft Exchange Server vulnerabilities (CVE-2021-26855 et al.) enabling pre-authentication RCE. Initially exploited exclusively by HAFNIUM, mass exploitation by at least 10 different APT groups followed Microsoft's March 2021 emergency patch.
campaigns.impact_summary: 250,000+ Exchange servers globally compromised; European Banking Authority email system breached; persistent web shell access maintained for months post-patch on unpatched systems

campaigns.name: Cl0p GoAnywhere MFT Exploitation
campaigns.description: Cl0p exploited a zero-day in Fortra's GoAnywhere MFT (CVE-2023-0669) for mass data exfiltration affecting 130+ organizations. The campaign preceded and foreshadowed the similar MOVEit campaign demonstrating Cl0p's focus on enterprise file transfer exploitation.
campaigns.impact_summary: 130+ organizations affected including Community Health Systems, Hatch Bank, Procter & Gamble, Hitachi, City of Toronto; millions of records exfiltrated

campaigns.name: Scattered Spider Casino Campaign
campaigns.description: Scattered Spider conducted back-to-back attacks against major casino/hospitality companies in late 2023. After Caesars (August) and MGM (September), the group demonstrated a pattern of targeting large leisure/entertainment conglomerates using consistent social engineering TTPs against IT help desks.
campaigns.impact_summary: Two of the largest casino operators in the US breached within weeks; combined estimated impact $115M+; FBI issued public advisory; demonstrated vulnerability of help-desk based identity verification

campaigns.name: Volt Typhoon Critical Infrastructure Pre-positioning
campaigns.description: Volt Typhoon has maintained persistent access to US critical infrastructure networks for years, believed to be pre-positioning for disruptive operations in the event of conflict over Taiwan. The group uses entirely living-off-the-land techniques and legitimate tools to avoid detection. CISA, NSA, and FBI issued a joint advisory in May 2023.
campaigns.impact_summary: Critical infrastructure in Guam and continental US compromised; persistent access maintained for potential disruptive operations; CISA/NSA/FBI joint advisory issued; China has denied involvement

campaigns.name: APT41 Healthcare Campaign
campaigns.description: APT41 targeted COVID-19 vaccine research and healthcare organizations during the pandemic, attempting to steal intellectual property related to vaccine development. The US DOJ indicted five Chinese nationals affiliated with APT41 in September 2020. This campaign demonstrated nation-state exploitation of healthcare crises.
campaigns.impact_summary: COVID-19 vaccine research targeted at multiple pharmaceutical companies; healthcare data theft; US DOJ indictment of 5 APT41-linked individuals in September 2020

campaigns.name: LockBit 3.0 Royal Mail Attack
campaigns.description: LockBit 3.0 encrypted Royal Mail's international shipping systems in January 2023, disrupting international parcel and letter services for weeks. LockBit initially demanded a $80M ransom, which Royal Mail refused. The group subsequently leaked 44GB of Royal Mail data on their darkweb site.
campaigns.impact_summary: Royal Mail international services disrupted for 6 weeks; 11.5 million parcels backlogged; $80M ransom demanded and refused; 44GB data leaked publicly; £10M+ estimated remediation costs
